# Image Labeling Guide

How to label and annotate container images so OCIDex can ingest them reliably and display rich metadata.

---

## How OCIDex reads image metadata

OCIDex reads image metadata at two points in the lifecycle:

**At ingest time (synchronous):** When an SBOM is generated by Syft (via registry scan, webhook, or direct upload), OCIDex reads `syft:image:*` properties embedded in the BOM. Syft sources these from the image **config labels** — `LABEL` instructions in your Dockerfile. Syft does not read manifest annotations.

**After ingest (async enrichment):** The OCI enricher contacts the registry directly and reads metadata from three sources, in priority order:

1. **Manifest annotations** — OCI manifest-level annotations
2. **Config labels** — `LABEL` instructions baked into the image
3. **Index annotations** — annotations on the parent image index (multi-arch manifests)

The practical consequence: **config labels are the authoritative source for ingest**. Manifest-only annotations will not be seen until the async enricher runs, and they cannot rescue a rejected ingest.

---

## Mandatory fields for container SBOMs

OCIDex rejects container SBOMs with HTTP 422 if any of these three fields cannot be resolved:

| Field | What it represents |
|---|---|
| `subject_version` | Human-readable image version (e.g. `v1.4.2`) |
| `architecture` | Target CPU architecture (e.g. `amd64`, `arm64`) |
| `build_date` | When the image was built (RFC 3339) |

**Architecture** is almost always handled automatically — Docker and BuildKit set the config blob `architecture` field to match the build target, and Syft reads it directly.

**`subject_version` and `build_date` must be set explicitly** as config labels. Without them, ingest fails.

After ingest, the async enricher also checks for **enrichment sufficiency**: both `imageVersion` and `architecture` must be present in the enrichment data. This is separate from the ingest validation and determines whether the SBOM is considered fully enriched in the UI.

---

## Quick start: Dockerfile labels

The fastest way to make your images work with OCIDex is to add two labels to your Dockerfile:

```dockerfile
ARG VERSION=dev
ARG BUILDTIME

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version="$VERSION"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created="$BUILDTIME"
```

Build with:

```bash
docker build \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 .
```

That's enough for successful ingest. The sections below cover how to provide richer metadata.

---

## Recommended labels

These labels populate the full set of metadata fields that OCIDex displays in the UI:

```dockerfile
ARG VERSION=dev
ARG BUILDTIME
ARG GIT_SHA

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.version="$VERSION"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.created="$BUILDTIME"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="my-service"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Short description of what this image does"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/myorg/myrepo"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.revision="$GIT_SHA"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.vendor="My Org"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="Apache-2.0"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.url="https://myorg.example.com/my-service"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.documentation="https://myorg.example.com/my-service/docs"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.authors="team@myorg.example.com"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.base.name="debian:bookworm-slim"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.base.digest="sha256:abc123..."
```

Build with:

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --build-arg GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

---

## Manifest annotations

Manifest annotations are set at the OCI manifest level, outside the image filesystem. They are read by the async OCI enricher (not by Syft), so they do not affect whether ingest succeeds.

Manifest annotations are useful for:
- Adding metadata to images you don't control the Dockerfile for
- Updating metadata after an image has been built
- Workflows where config labels are impractical

### Setting manifest annotations with buildx

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --annotation "org.opencontainers.image.version=v1.4.2" \
  --annotation "org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com/myorg/myrepo" \
  --annotation "org.opencontainers.image.description=My service" \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

### Setting manifest annotations with oras

You can use `oras` to attach or modify annotations on images already in a registry:

```bash
# Copy an image and add annotations
oras copy myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --to-plain-http

# Attach annotations to an existing manifest
oras manifest annotate myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  org.opencontainers.image.version=v1.4.2 \
  org.opencontainers.image.source=https://github.com/myorg/myrepo
```

### Priority when both are set

When the same annotation key exists as both a manifest annotation and a config label, the **manifest annotation wins** in the enricher output (which is what appears in the UI once enrichment completes).

---

## Index annotations (multi-arch images)

For multi-arch images (image indices/manifest lists), OCIDex also reads annotations from the parent image index. Index annotations serve as a fallback when a field is not present in either the manifest annotations or config labels.

### Setting index annotations with buildx

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
  --annotation "index:org.opencontainers.image.description=My multi-arch service" \
  --annotation "index:org.opencontainers.image.licenses=Apache-2.0" \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

The `index:` prefix tells buildx to place the annotation on the image index rather than on individual platform manifests.

### How index lookup works

The enricher discovers the parent index by looking up the image's version as a tag. It checks these sources in order:

1. The SBOM's `subject_version`
2. The config label `org.opencontainers.image.version`
3. The config label `org.label-schema.version`
4. The bare config label `version`

If the version resolves to an image index (not a single manifest), the index's annotations are used as the lowest-priority fallback source.

---

## Multi-arch builds

For multi-arch images, OCIDex's registry catalog scan expands the image index and creates a separate SBOM for each platform manifest, each with its own architecture.

```bash
docker buildx build \
  --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
  --build-arg VERSION=v1.4.2 \
  --build-arg BUILDTIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  -t myregistry.example.com/my-service:v1.4.2 \
  --push .
```

Config labels set in the Dockerfile apply to every platform manifest. Index annotations apply once at the index level and are inherited as fallback metadata by all platform-specific SBOMs.

---

## Metadata priority chain

The enricher resolves each metadata field using the first non-empty value from this priority chain:

```
manifest annotations > config labels > index annotations
```

For fields with legacy equivalents, fallback keys are also checked within each source:

| Field | OCI key | Legacy / bare fallbacks |
|---|---|---|
| `imageVersion` | `org.opencontainers.image.version` | `org.label-schema.version`, `version` |
| `sourceUrl` | `org.opencontainers.image.source` | `org.label-schema.vcs-url`, `vcs-url` |
| `revision` | `org.opencontainers.image.revision` | `org.label-schema.vcs-ref`, `vcs-ref` |
| `description` | `org.opencontainers.image.description` | `org.label-schema.description`, `description` |
| `title` | `org.opencontainers.image.title` | `org.label-schema.name`, `name` |
| `vendor` | `org.opencontainers.image.vendor` | `org.label-schema.vendor`, `vendor` |
| `url` | `org.opencontainers.image.url` | `org.label-schema.url`, `url` |
| `documentation` | `org.opencontainers.image.documentation` | `org.label-schema.usage`, `usage` |
| `created` | (config blob `created` timestamp) | `org.label-schema.build-date`, `build-date` |
| `licenses` | `org.opencontainers.image.licenses` | — |
| `authors` | `org.opencontainers.image.authors` | — |
| `baseName` | `org.opencontainers.image.base.name` | — |
| `baseDigest` | `org.opencontainers.image.base.digest` | — |
| `refName` | `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` | — |

`architecture` and `os` are always read from the config blob — they are not sourced from annotations or labels.

The `created` timestamp uses a separate priority: the config blob's `created` field takes precedence over any label or annotation value.

---

## Label reference

All labels are optional except `version` and `created` for successful ingest (see [Mandatory fields](#mandatory-fields-for-container-sboms)).

| Label / annotation key | OCIDex field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `org.opencontainers.image.version` | `imageVersion`, `subject_version` | **Required for ingest.** Human-readable version tag. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.created` | `build_date` | **Required for ingest.** RFC 3339 timestamp (e.g. `2024-03-15T10:30:00Z`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.title` | `title` | Short display name for the image. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.description` | `description` | One-line description. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.source` | `sourceUrl` | URL to source repository. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.revision` | `revision` | Git commit SHA or tag. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.vendor` | `vendor` | Organization or team name. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.licenses` | `licenses` | SPDX license expression (e.g. `Apache-2.0`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.url` | `url` | Project or product homepage. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.documentation` | `documentation` | Documentation URL. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.authors` | `authors` | Contact information. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.base.name` | `baseName` | Base image name (e.g. `debian:bookworm-slim`). |
| `org.opencontainers.image.base.digest` | `baseDigest` | Digest of the base image. |
| `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` | `refName` | Reference name (e.g. a tag like `v3.18`). |

These keys work as config labels (Dockerfile `LABEL`), manifest annotations, or index annotations. The priority chain described above determines which source wins when the same key appears in multiple places.

---

## Legacy label-schema support

OCIDex supports the deprecated [label-schema](http://label-schema.org/) convention as a fallback. If OCI standard keys are not present, these are checked:

| Legacy key | Maps to |
|---|---|
| `org.label-schema.version` | `imageVersion` |
| `org.label-schema.build-date` | `created` |
| `org.label-schema.vcs-url` | `sourceUrl` |
| `org.label-schema.vcs-ref` | `revision` |
| `org.label-schema.description` | `description` |
| `org.label-schema.name` | `title` |
| `org.label-schema.vendor` | `vendor` |
| `org.label-schema.url` | `url` |
| `org.label-schema.usage` | `documentation` |

Bare (unnamespaced) keys like `version`, `name`, `vendor` are also supported as a final fallback.

New images should use the `org.opencontainers.image.*` namespace.

---

## Direct API upload

If you upload a Syft-generated SBOM directly via `POST /api/v1/sbom` without the config labels above, you can supply the missing fields as query parameters:

```bash
syft myregistry.example.com/my-service@sha256:abc123 -o cyclonedx-json | \
  curl -X POST "https://ocidex.example.com/api/v1/sbom?version=v1.4.2&architecture=amd64&build_date=2024-03-15T10:30:00Z" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d @-
```

Query parameters override any values extracted from the BOM and are the most reliable option when labels are not present. The async OCI enricher still runs after ingest and will populate additional metadata fields from the registry.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Ingest rejected with 422

The error response lists which mandatory fields are missing. Common causes:

- **Missing `subject_version`**: No `org.opencontainers.image.version` label in the Dockerfile, and no `version` query parameter on the API call.
- **Missing `build_date`**: No `org.opencontainers.image.created` label. Add it to the Dockerfile or pass `build_date` as a query parameter.
- **Missing `architecture`**: Rare — usually means the image was built with an unusual tool that doesn't set the config blob architecture. Pass `architecture` as a query parameter.

### Enrichment shows incomplete metadata

After ingest, the OCI enricher runs asynchronously. If metadata fields are still empty:

- Verify the image is accessible from the OCIDex server's network.
- Check that the registry does not require authentication that OCIDex is not configured with.
- For index annotations: ensure the version label/tag resolves to an image index in the registry. The enricher looks up `{repo}:{version}` — if that tag doesn't exist or points to a single manifest instead of an index, index annotations won't be fetched.

### Manifest annotations not appearing

Manifest annotations are only read by the async enricher, not at ingest time. If you set annotations but don't see them:

- Wait for enrichment to complete (check the SBOM detail in the UI).
- Verify the annotations are on the manifest (not the index) using `oras manifest fetch <image>` or `crane manifest <image>`.
